segment-text: NIGHTMARE ALLEY
Guillermo del Toro & Kim Morgan
Final Shooting Script - December 1, 2021

DISPOSAL OF THIS SCRIPT COPY DOES NOT ALTER ANY OF THE RESTRICTIONS SET FORTH ABOVE. Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
INT. COUNTRY SHACK - DUSK

A lean man in his thirties, STANTON "STAN" CARLISLE drags a burlap-wrapped corpse into an open grave- in the center of the room, made with missing floorboards. A CAN OF GASOLINE is lined up in front of the grave. STAN, hat and coat on, lights his cigarette- throws the match. Everything catches fire. Stan picks up his radio and suitcase, and walks out the door.
EXT. DESOLATE LANDSCAPE/SHACK - DUSK
The shack burns. Stan walks away.
INT. BUS - MOVING - DUSK
Stan examines his wristwatch. Folds a handkerchief, puts it in his hat. He covers his face with his hat and goes to sleep. The light changes around him. Becomes night.
INT. BUS - NIGHT
A CARNIVAL can be seen and heard in the distance. The bus stops. STAN wakes up, looks around.
Alright folks, last stop, end of the
line... Grab your belongings and head off the bus, thank you. STAN picks up his belongings, exits the bus.
EXT. BUS STOP CAFE - CONTINUOUS

STAN heads towards the CAFE. Out of the cafe emerges a diminutive man (THE MAJOR) who is carrying a take out bag- He walks by, THE MAJOR looks at STANSTAN follows him as he enters a dirt road through the woods. Beyond: LIGHTS AND COLOR!! DISTANT CALLIOPE MUSIC!!
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 EXT. CARNIVAL GROUNDS - NIGHT

STAN, hat on, carrying his radio and suitcase, entersfollowing THE MAJOR. Calliope music and rancid cotton candy smell fills the air- all around us faded banners advertise marvels from far away. Merry-go-round, Ferris Wheel and bumper carsCARNIVAL BARKER (O.C.)
greatest carnival in the world! Spine
Hurry, hurry, hurry! Behold the beauties

The crowd is dwindling. A TENT OF STRIPPERS dressed in tantalizing orientalist rags. Stan follows THE MAJOR, who climbs onto a stage announcing ELECTRA- THE AMAZING ELECTRICAL GIRL. POV: MOLLY, A WOMAN IN HER MID-TWENTIES, and BRUNO, an aging strong man, walk off the stage as a group of WORKMEN "fold" the attraction.
Hey, look what I got for ya - chocolate!
THE MAJOR gives MOLLY a bar of chocolate.
(to Molly) You ready to go? We got the sandwiches.
She puts on a raincoat and they all head outSTAN sees the trio disappear behind a tentBARKER (CLEM) ... one last chance- to witness this
to remember that this exhibit is being

The Barker is CLEM HOATLEY, a middle aged man- once charming, seductive even, now ravaged by the carnival, soiled by it. He smokes and barks to a SMALL CROWD- uses his cigarette like a baton. He enters the tent.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
STAN blends in with the crowd and follows.
INT. TEN-IN-ONE - GEEK PIT - CONTINUOUS
A big tent with a large excavated PIT.
This creature has been examined by the
Unequivocally a manThe crowd looks down- a door opens in the side of the pit. A figure moves in the shadows- a dirty human shape with a mop of matted hair.
He can go for weeks with neither food nor drink and live entirely on the atmosphere. But you are in luck, because tonight we're going to feed him one last time! (to Louise) Peach...

Nearby, LOUISE (CLEM'S wife)- very pregnant- nods to Clem and walks off holding a GRIMY WICKER BASKET at the end of a stick. He reaches down- he opens a small wooden cage and from it he draws a half-grown leghorn pullet, complaining. He holds it up so the crowd can see it.
There will be a slight additional charge for this attraction but it's not a dollar, not fifty cents, but a quarter! One fourth of a dollar and

you will see the Geek feed- suckle on the blood of reptiles and birds- like a babe feeding on its mother's milk! CLEM releases the chickenThe necks crane down. A GRUNT of anticipation raises from the cage. TOP SHOT: the circle of the geek enclosureThe chicken clucks around near the immobile GEEK. Nothing occurs. A beat and then-
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21

THE GEEK moves, lightning fast (!), towards the chicken, and snatches it- The GEEK howls and howls in the darknessThe crowd cheers the Geek on. THE GEEK BITES THE NECK OF THE BIRD- an explosion of blood! The crowd reacts to the feeding in the cage.
Come on... fourth of a dollar...
Louise walks around, collecting money with the basket.
Twenty-five cents... pay up...
STAN ducks awayCLEM spots StanSTAN moves past JARS OF PICKLED PUNKS, exits the tent into8
EXT. TENT ALLEY - CONTINUOUS
TENT ALLEY: The Carnies' "Inner Sanctum." THERE ARE BOXES, CRATES AND OPEN PORTABLE DRESSERS. THE MAJOR and BRUNO are sitting at a table, smoking stogies. STAN looks aroundconfused.
Hey, Mack- you can't be back here.
The Major stands up, walks towards Stan.
You... you're the cluck from the bus stopYou followed me here, didn't ya? Why?
BRUNO approaches.
What's your pitch, Pal? You on the level? You need work?
He's just peddlin' applesauceBRUNO Major... Major, gimme a minute. Alright, we got a storm coming through- I gotta load out fast and I'm short a few guysIt's hard work. You get a dollar- you
move on- No moochin' around- Savvy?
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 EXT. CARNIVAL GROUNDS - NIGHT

Now it's raining like hell. A deluge. STAN helps load heavy wooden poles into a truckA crew (led by BRUNO) dismantles the main tentBRUNO Alright friends - on the count of three. Ready... one, two, three... let her down! -STAN helps. The entire carnival is being taken apart.
EXT. EMPTY GROUNDS - PAYMENT TENT - NIGHT
Rain has waned to a drizzle. Trucks are moving. Stan stands at the end of the line and gets paid in by CLEM. Stan examines the coins in his hand.
Want I should help you count it, Rockefeller?
It was a dollar - Minus the till for the
'At's right- I clocked you, cluck.
STAN moves away.
Clem walks towards STANCLEM (CONT'D) I'll give you five for the radio.

BRUNO closes the TALLY and a TIN BOX with the moneyClem produces a five dollar bill from a THICK ROLL of moneyCLEM (CONT'D) Tell you what: We're joining another carnival about twenty miles down the roadI'll give you the five- plus a nice, hot meal when we get thereStan nods.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 EXT. BUNGALOWS AND EMPTY LOT - NIGHT
The trucks arrive at the edge of a small town- park amidst a small dusty field- next to low rent bungalows. PART OF THE CARNIVAL is already up. STAN climbs out of the truck.
INT. REFECTORY TENT - NIGHT

Under a dingy tarp, Stan takes some cornbread, beans, coffee and eggs. RAIN PICKS UP. A guy, FUNHOUSE JACK (lean, 30's, in tank top and hat) strums the blues on his guitar- SNAKE MAN (a tall, lanky African American in his early 30's) by his sideBRUNO and The Major are having breakfast with MOLLY. Molly stands up, walks out of the tent. She glances at Stan as she passes. Stan looks up and watches her go. A whistle!! Many of the Carny folk move away in a hurryCLEM Come on, fellas! I need help! Chop chop! It's CLEM- handing BATONS and FLASHLIGHTS to WORKERS.
Hey! You! Radio Flyer- come here!
STAN goes to CLEM.
The Geek- he got loose- if you see him, don't take him on your own.
STAN is handed a flashlight, a whistle and a batonCLEM (CONT'D)
EXT. TENT ENCAMPMENT - NIGHT
STAN, CLEM, BRUNO, SNAKE MAN, and OTHERS, move through the tents, flashlights in hand.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
They use their batons to beat the tarps- their flashlights to illuminate the undercarriages.
He's under the truck!
Stan and Clem look under the truck.
No, keep going! To the funhouse!
Stan runs off.
EXT. FUNHOUSE - NIGHT
STAN approaches the FunhouseFUNHOUSE JACK runs up.
Hurry! I'll let you in. FUNHOUSE JACK climbs up- pushes the pedal and opens the doors.
INT. FUNHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
They enter. FUNHOUSE JACK pushes a pedal- The doors close behind them. Rain LEAKS everywhere- offering little protection against the downpour. They stand in the darkness. Noises.
You go in there. If you see him, push him through(beat)

I'll meet you the other sideFUNHOUSE JACK turns on the attraction. ALL THE FIGURES AND LIGHTS ACTIVATE!!! Funhouse Jack leaves through the doors. STAN moves through the whirling barrel-the HALL OF MIRRORSA noise- Stan turns around-
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
A GIANT DEVIL'S FACE opens its jaws wide, revealing an entrance. Stan walks into Hell-spots the GEEK crouched in the corner-A thin man, wiry, wild-eyed- CHEST COVERED IN SCARS!!
Hey, Pal- Everybody's lookin for you- I'm not going to blow the whistle, you didn't do nothing against me. Why don't you come on out? Come on, I'm not going to hurt you.
I'mnotlikethisI'mnotlikethisI'mnotlikethis...
STAN looks at the geek- his eyes- slightly misaligned and- at one corner of his mouth- A GLINT- a gold tooth. The Geek picks up a rock.
GEEK (CONT'D)

ImnotlikethisThe GEEK attacks Stan, bashing his head with the rockknocking him down. The GEEK makes a dash for the exitDoors open: CLEM shows upCLEM Easy! EasyThe GEEK turns and runs towards STAN- STAN takes him down and thrashes him, hits him twice- hardCLEM (CONT'D) Hey! Hey! You're gonna kill him! STAN turns around- mad eyes.
Jesus, KidClem kneels next to the Geek. FUNHOUSE JACK arrives.
Oh, Jesus, is he dead? I don't need no shooflies in here, Clem- get him out of here-
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 INT. TEN-IN-ONE - NIGHT
STAN and CLEM drag the GEEK into the cage.

-in the back... in the cage, in the backHe starts coming to. They hurry out as the Geek howls, gnashes his teeth, twists. He charges against the menCLEM (CONT'D) Out! Out! Go! Go! Go! -CLEM closes the barred door just in time. The Geek slams his head against the metal bars, extends his hand- stretching it!!
Back! Back! Back! Back!
Clem hits him with the baton and the Geek falls back. The Geek BLEEDS profusely and whimpers like a beaten dog.
"... Step right up and behold- one of the unexplained mysteries of the
universe. Is he man or beast?..."
ImnotlikethisImnotlikethis...
Clem locks the cage. Stan lights a cigarette with a STRIKE MATCH (using his thumb to light it). Takes a puff. CLEM takes a pocket hankie and cleans his shoes.
Folks'll pay good money just to make 'emselves feel better. Look down on this fucker grind some chicken gristle. (beat)
How's your head? TurnStan does. Clem checks his bleeding scalp- Stan flinches away from himClem motions past the JARS OF PICKLED PUNKS.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
You can find a mattress over there behind those jars. You can sleep there tonight. Stay out of the rain.
Stan walks through.
Go on, go on - around the corner.
Stan walks around the corner, where there's a small cot.
Would a steady job be of interest to you, Young Buck? Folks here, they don't make no nevermind who you are and what you done.
Clem walks away, leaving Stan.
INT. SHACK (DREAM)
The shack is on fire. Flames in reverse.
INT. SHACK - DUSK (FLASHBACK)
Laid out in bed, an infirm, old man. Pale and sweaty. Stan sits on the bed next to him. They look at each other-19
INT. TEN-IN-ONE - GEEK PIT - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
The Geek clutches the chicken, prepares to bite20
INT. TEN-IN-ONE - DAWN
Stan wakes up, agitated. Light of day pours in. He gets up and exits his sleeping area- and into the21
EXT. CARNIVAL GROUNDS - WASHING AREA / REFECTORY - CONTINUOUS 21

A thick mist rises from the ground: STAN stands in line to use a grimy barrel of water to bathe... THE DOGBOY washes, shirtless. Nearby, SNAKE MAN rehearses with FUNHOUSE JACK playingMOLLY sits on the stage, watching them. Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 STAN walks over to watch. Hey.
(to Snake Man) Look at you go.
They finish the number and Molly claps. She stands up and walks away. STAN watches her go22
EXT. BUNGALOWS NEAR ENCAMPMENT - DAY

STAN walks around, spotsZEENA - a striking but time-worn bleach blonde of indeterminate age (one would guess, mid-forties but her eyes seem much wiser) and faded glamour - on the porch, listening to a RADIO, and reading a FILM MAGAZINE... Stan approachesMornin'.
ZEENA
Do you got a tub?
She nods. Fans herself with the FILM MAGAZINE.
I certainly do.
Just arrived last night, so I thought...
Of course you did. Well, it'd be a dime to use it. You get a cake of soap and hot water.
STAN puts out his cigarette.
INT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - CONTINUOUS
They enter.
Leave the door open?
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Sure. But put that dime on the table.
Zeena walks into the bedroom, wakes up PETE, a distinguished, if modestly-dressed, man in his 60's.
Sorry to wake you, Sugar- He's just getting a soakStan puts the dime on the table. Spots TAROT CARDS.
I gotta go into town anyway(to Stan) Morning(to Zeena) And I'm going to see 'bout getting some breakfast.
Now sweetie- Make sure it's breakfast, ok? Promise Zeena you
won't go into some blind pigShe rubs his shoulder. He smiles but his eyes are full of pain.
(to Stan) You familiar with those cards? Tarot, very powerful stuff. Maybe Zeena will read your fortune, if you're lucky. After your bath.
PETE locks eyes with him, briefly- and produces a cigarette out of thin air. Smiles and leaves... Stan enters the bathroom, Zeena runs the water for him.
You can take all your things off, and hang 'em over the chair there, nice and
I'll have Eggs a la Benedict, a
croissant, jus d'orange et cafe au lait, and uh... bah-boom! Zeena removes her slipper.
Au revoir ma cherie!
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Pete exits, closes the door behind him.
INT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - DAY
Stan, naked, lights a cigarette. Gets in the bath. Zeena UNPACKS and arranges a few porcelain figurines on a mantle.
I'm gonna help Clem a bitClem, huh?
Zeena goes to the table.
Say maybe you can hawk for me- you know, work the audience? We got a little mindreading show.
What do I have to do?
The TAROT cards: one is unturned- she flips it: THE STAR She smiles.

Ah, aint nothing to it- Carny speak's like a song- We sound Southern to Southerners, and Western to Westerners. You gotta sweet lil' lilt: just got to lean on it. She gets the soap and gets the suds going.

Talk of the soil. You drawl slow, but all the while you're hustling fast. Yeah, you can peddle for me... And on your downtimemaybe Pete can show you a trick or two. You're going to do just fine, honey. You have panache...
What's that?
Oh, you're easy on the eyes, Honey.
They stare at each other-
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Oh, you're trouble, ain't you, pup?
Stan leans forward.
No, ma'am: I'm absolutely no trouble at all.
Zeena laughs- a younger laughter.
Oh, yes you are- You're a "maybe"- And "maybes" are real bad for me.
She raises herself on her elbows and lets her hair fall down on each side of his face and kisses him lightly. She soaps her hand, goes underwater... and strokes Stan.
Ain't you the one- I better go...
She takes the cigarette out of his mouth.
Yeah, just try 'n get away, you dopeShe takes a drag.
They kiss passionately25
EXT. CARNIVAL GROUNDS - DUSK
BRUNO lifts a barbell above his head- it's marked: "500 pounds". It's obviously less... The crowd applauds. BRUNO leaves the stage. THE MAJOR is there in military tights.
Take over, willya? My knees are shot to shit...
Always with the knees...
THE MAJOR climbs in on the canvas.
I am The Major- tiniest man on recordmaster of Nami-Jitsu, the secret technique from the Orient. (MORE)
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
And twenty dollars say that I can beat any of you yahoos- in a wrestling match26
EXT. CARNIVAL GROUNDS - ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
Zeena's on stage with her crystal ball.
So- Don't be bashful, folks: If there's any of you that have a question for me, Mr. Carlisle will now be collecting your cards and envelopes.
Stan circulates amongst the crowd with a basket.
Make sure to write your question and your name on the card inside the envelope- An' don't look at anybody else's- Let's all mind our matters and we'll stay out of trouble, now.
He spots MOLLY amidst the crowd.
Miss Molly, you have a question for us? No.
Well, you're not going to get an answer then.
Stan tips his hat at her. She smiles.
There are five senses we all possess: we all can hear, see, touch, smell and tastehowever... I have been blessed with a sixth one: a natural connection, if you will, to the ether.
Stan carries the basket to the back of the stage.
Thank you for the envelopes, Mr CarlisleShe grabs an EXACT REPLICA of the basket, complete with envelopes, whileStan skillfully ducks into a small niche below it.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 INT. UNDER ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
There is Pete, sleeping. GEORGE the rabbit nearbyA few empty bottles are scattered around.
Zeena's cooking up a good crowd out there, Pete... you sauced? Come on Pete, Pete- Zeena's on!
Stan dumps the envelopes at his feet. Gives Pete the small blackboard.
I'll read the first card. She's going to need it soon. Okay, Abigail, write it down, where's the chalk?
Right here... Abigail...
Pete starts writing on the blackboard.
EXT. ZEENA'S STAGE - SAME
Now then, people always ask me if I have spirit aid.
Zeena raises a bottle of alcohol.
Well folks, the only spirits I control are the ones in this bottle-- spirits of alcohol.
She pours the alcohol into the bowl. INT. UNDER ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
Worried about her mother. Going to be looking soon.
Pete writes on the blackboard, circles the A in Abigail.
And every card after that, you understand?
I know it, I know it.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21 EXT. ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
Zeena throws a match and- FWOOOSH! The crowd gasps! STAN gets a box full of amulets and pamphlets and hits the crowd...
The questions are now in the ether: I've never touched them. Never read them- I don't need to because I get an impression right away.
Zeena strokes her forehead, covering her eyes with her handlooking down31
INT. UNDER ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
-Pete puts the blackboard on a stand - says "ABIGAIL WORRIED, MOTHER, $".
There you go, Zeenie.
Looks up: A glass cutout makes it visible from stage.
EXT. ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
There's a woman- worried about her mother. 'Will mother get better?'
Stan walks over to Molly.
Pete's sauced. I'll go.
Wait, I'm getting a letter- yes it's an "A".
A PALE WOMAN (Farmer's wife, Sunday's best, ten years out of style) takes two steps forward and raises her hand. That's me! PALE WOMAN
Abigail- is that your name, dear?
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
She nods, the crowds sucks in its collective breath!!! INT. UNDER ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS

I'd say your mother has had a lot of hard work in her life and she's had a lot of trouble, mostly about money. Am I correct? I know how it is folks, 'cause I've had my share of troubles, same as all of you...
Molly enters.
MOLLY looks for the letterMOLLY Pete- Pete- which is the envelope? Is it
I can't remember. I'm sorry Molly, I can't...
Now I will attempt to read the next question.
MOLLY signals ZEENA: "NOTHING HERE" EXT. ZEENA'S STAGE - CONTINUOUS
Madam Zeena, tell me more, pleaseZEENA (improvises) You... have a couple of brothers- or sisters, haven't you?
No. I don't, ma'am. Only one sister. My
brother passed awayThe crowd chuckles- some heckle. The smile on ZEENA's face flickers. Stan watches- tense-
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Ah, yes- but I see him- that is what threw me- your brother- he is right here this very minute- His hand- is on your shoulder- can you feel it?
Yes- yes- I felt something- I felt his
hand- Oh God, Harry! The crowd Oohs! And Aahs! STAN smiles. Applause"Umbrella Man" rises in the soundtrack.
EXT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - NIGHT
It's raining. Zeena stands talking to the Pale Woman under umbrellas.
I'm sorry, I can't...
Zeena hands something to her. The Pale Woman walks away and Zeena goes inside.
INT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - CONTINUOUS
The song emanates from a radio. PETE pets George on his lap. Stan counts the money collected that day, tallies it up. ZEENA walks back in- shuffles the Tarot cards. You okay?
The poor woman. She wanted to know more...
'Course she did. What did you tell her?
The truth. That we missed a cue and that I had to use a trick to ease the crowd. That it was just a trick. It hurtPETE Well, it's good. It's good that you did that.
Yeah, well. I gave her an amulet.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
I'm sorry I missed the cue- ISTAN I don't know why you don't keep it going for herPETE Never do a spook show. No good comes out of a spook show.
Is it so bad to give her hope?
Aint hope if it's a lie, Stan.
Alright, what are those cards, then- the Tarot?
Oh, Honey- it's not the same thing- I even run 'em over for myself- and Pete. You always get some kind of an answer.
Zeena and Pete exchange a long, loving, painful look. PETE takes her hand.
I bought those for you. In Paris.
We stayed at the Ritz.
Zeena scratches his backPETE Held over: four weeks- Mon petite SourisI wrote most of the word system on that
Can you still do it?

Pete looks at Zeena- she brushes hair from his forehead, giving the go-ahead. Pete turns around in his SWIVEL CHAIR, covers his eyes. His movements become stylized, elegant- as if in a stage under spotlight.
Alright. Stan- Give Zeena an article- any
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Stan chooses his watch.
Please concentrate on this object I have in my hand, Professor.
Pete extends his right hand dramatically. He takes the index of his left to his temple.
What can you tell me?
Wrist watch, leather band, brass, not gold - old and worn down- but it's full of meaningStan freezes.
The watch: It wasn't yours- originallywas it? You took it- or stole it, didn't you(beat) I see an older man- the boy hates him.
The boy would love to be loved, but he
He turns. Stan's eyes are fixated on Pete.
He loved that watch... it was his pride. My father.
Pete laughs- snaps his fingers.
Was it now? Well, my boy- That's itPete pulls out a small black notebook from his pocket. Tosses it at Stan: in it are words, numbers and a FOLDOUT diagram. Zeena hands Stan his watch.

It's all there. Each word has a number and that number takes you to a different word- Ba-bum- Zeena feeds them to me: Please concentrate on the object I have in my hand, Professor. 'Please' and 'object', twenty-oneemphasis on "concentrate" means it's
old, worn down... Cheap. Ba-bum.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
(beat) But you gotta know how to read the mark: how they move, talk- dress... People are desperate to tell you who they are, desperate to be seen...
Ain't that the truth.
Yeah but how do you know you got 'em hooked?
You pause.
That's rightPete retrieves the code book, pockets it.
You take a moment- "would you excuse me please...? "I need a glass of water."
Or you faint- weakened by the effort...
How did you know...? About my father?
Stan is on edge.

Stock reading, "Black Rainbow"- One size fits all: "You're thrifty but generous- Private but friendly- You hated him but loved him..." (beat) Everybody's had some trouble. Somebody they hated. Shadow from their pastStan lights a cigarette.

Usually for a boy, it's the old man. If the mark is older, then you say "You had some present loss". Younger? A father. There is always a father. Pete stubs out his cigarette and takes the receipts and money.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
Well, I'll take this to Clem. We had a good day today... A great day.
He opens the door, bows and exits.
My sweet Pete. He breaks my heart.
EXT. TEN-IN-ONE - NIGHT
RAIN has stopped. PETE talks to Clem.
No- no- no more beltin' the grape for ya, Pete. I promised ZeenaPETE Zeena's not going to know, Clem. Just one
bottle, that's allClem hands him a quart.
Ok. Last time. Just go.
Bless you. Bless you.
CAMERA discovers Stan, taking the scene in.
INT. TEN-IN-ONE - PICKLED PUNKS - NIGHT
Clem pulls up his suspenders. He points at the rows and rows of deformed fetuses in jars of all kinds- floating in Amber liquid. STAN walks in behind him holding a PIG FETUS IN A JAR.
These are my Lil' Angels(points to a table) Set it down here, that's his home.
Stan sets it down.
No, facing the other way, he's shy. Gotta keep my beauties pickled fresh.
Stan turns the bottle.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21

"Begotten by the same lust and sweat that got us all walkin' on this earth- but gone wrong somehow in the maternal womb... Not fit for living..." Most of them die right at childbirth, or even inside the mother. Now this one is a rare one...
He points at a LARGE JAR with a CYCLOPS baby. STAN reads a label, written on a grimy sticker at the base of the Jar: ENOCH.

Enoch- I named him on accounta the bibleLil' fucker killed his mother right at childbirth- flopped around for a couple of days- braying like a calf. (beat) Eye follows you around like 'em portraitsClem opens a trunk: lined up with quarter glass bottles.
CLEM (CONT'D)
Christ all mighty, throat's sore as a bull's ass in fly time.
He drinks- Stan declines.
Folks know to come to me for it. Half a plug a quart- Never cop a quart- you gotta make the tin cling- same as
everybody elseHe shakes a metal box full of coins- chained to the table.
I never touch the stuff.
Ain't we virtuous?! Just say you understand.
I understand.
Nightmare Alley - FINAL SHOOTING SCRIPT - 12-1-21
What do you understand?
Never cop a quart.
That's right: Toe the line- Don't fuck with me. That's the lesson.
EXT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - DAY
Stan walks towards the bungalow carrying a small WOODEN BOX with six quarts of alcohol.
INT. ZEENA'S BUNGALOW - DAY
PETE alone in the bungalow. He arranges amulets for sale on a vending box. Next to him: a wicker basket and some envelopes. The door is ajar. Stan knocks on the doorframe. Entrer-
STAN enters with the box.
Clem asked me to drop this to you... six quarts of wood alcohol, for the show...
